mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances during walk-forward testing, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 9%, persistent labor market slack reduces household income growth and dampens aggregate consumption forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate a coordinated expansion of final demand and supply output, amplifying corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and INDPRO does not significantly predict positive 20-day forward returns (interaction term F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series effects over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery regimes, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal a bottoming in residential construction activity and cyclical demand revival, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST during post-recession periods do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a premature monetary tightening scare that raises near-term discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and FEDFUNDS increased versus prior month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered instances are not negative in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is not negative with magnitude above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) signals near-term manufacturing contraction and falling capacity utilization, which reduces earnings guidance and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion and declining recession probability, which lifts broad corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of quarters during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals reflation that supports nominal revenue growth and reduces real debt burdens for leveraged corporations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration does not predict positive 20-day forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
